rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add explicit battery efficiency and initial SoC in Inputs (or document sensitivity to those parameters) and annotate the exact formulas used in the spreadsheet cells (e.g., show formula text or ensure formula cells are not replaced by values).
rubric_item_scores.notes: Numeric values in ops_note are internally consistent and demonstrate correct unit conversions and arithmetic: totals of loads (39+51+23=113 W), minute->hour conversions (53 min = 0.8833 h, 41 min = 0.6833 h), sunlit generation = 97 W * 0.8833 h = ~85.68 Wh, sunlit load energy = 113 W * 0.8833 h = ~99.82 Wh, eclipse energy = 113 W * 0.6833 h = ~77.22 Wh, orbit battery draw = 14.13+77.22 = 91.35 Wh, DoD = 91.35/270 = 33.83%. Minimum sunlit array power for 30% DoD computed correctly (108.72 W). Missing battery-efficiency/initial-SoC inputs are flagged as needs_review rather than invented.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Add formulas or references in the sensitivity sheet that point directly to the base input cells (e.g., use =Inputs!C4*(1+perturbation)) to ensure full reproducibility; consider adding a short note explaining whether sunlit duration was held constant when perturbing eclipse duration.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Validation checks report an energy-residual of 0.000000 Wh and correctly flag DoD > 30% as FAIL. Sensitivity analysis is provided for two key inputs (solar array power and eclipse duration) including +/-10% and +/-20% perturbations, with resultant battery draw and DoD computed and cited. Perturbations are applied reproducibly (percent change applied to base cells) and results are consistent with arithmetic.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: If available, include EPS conversion/derating and explicit usable battery fraction (e.g., end-of-discharge/temperature derating) as alternate scenarios rather than assuming full 270 Wh usable; cite those cells in Calculations for traceability.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Numeric calculations in ops_note match correct unit conversions and arithmetic: total load 113 W, sunlit energy = 97 W * (53/60) = 85.6833 Wh, eclipse energy = 113 W * (41/60) = 77.2167 Wh, total battery use = 91.35 Wh, eclipse-only DoD = 77.2167/270 = 28.60%, orbit DoD = 91.35/270 = 33.83% (FAIL). Missing operational inputs are flagged needs_review rather than fabricated.
